metadata.author: John
metadata.subject: The Fellowship of the Divine Life.

1: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes... concerning the Word of life;
That which we have seen and heard we report also to you that you also may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father and with His Son Jesus Christ.
If we confess our sins, He is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
2: And as for you, the anointing which you have received from Him abides in you, and you have no need that anyone teach you; but as His anointing teaches you concerning all things and is true and is not a lie, and even as it has taught you, abide in Him.
3: Behold what manner of love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God; and we are.

3.text: Fellowship is the flow of the divine life within the Body of Christ. It is the joint participation and common enjoyment of the Triune God.

9.text: God is 'faithful' to His word and 'righteous' because of the blood of Jesus. Based on Christ's finished work, God is legally bound to forgive us when we confess.

27.text: The anointing is the moving and working of the Holy Spirit within our spirit, teaching us the will of God subjectively by the 'tinting' of the divine element.
